title: subrisia petiolaris
intro: Subrisia petiolaris, also known as the flowering ash, is a plant species native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Subrisia petiolaris include the treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory infections, as well as its use as an antiseptic to prevent infection. Herbal preparations of Subrisia petiolaris include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
